baseline_leading_explanation: Amyloid/tau cascade with biomarker-defined AD biology and immune/vascular/metabolic modifiers.

hypotheses.claim_boundary: Amyloid and tau are central source-linked disease signals, but not a complete etiology.
hypotheses.title: Amyloid/tau cascade as central disease biology
weaknesses: Clinical benefit from amyloid removal remains modest and trial-dependent.
Tau and synaptic injury correlate closely with impairment but do not settle upstream causality.

hypotheses.claim_boundary: Inflammation and glial activation are repeatedly implicated but can be protective, harmful, or stage-dependent.
hypotheses.title: Immune-inflammatory and glial dysfunction

hypotheses.claim_boundary: Vascular pathology is source-linked and clinically relevant, but baseline treats it mostly as co-factor.
hypotheses.title: Vascular and blood-brain barrier contributions
weaknesses: Baseline under-separates vascular amyloid/ARIA safety from generic vascular risk.

hypotheses.claim_boundary: Energy, oxidative, insulin, and mitochondrial signals are supported, but causal ordering is weak.
hypotheses.title: Metabolic, mitochondrial, and oxidative stress pathways
weaknesses: Human interventional translation is not settled.

hypotheses.claim_boundary: APOE and genetic risk alter disease vulnerability across amyloid, lipid, immune, and vascular axes.
hypotheses.title: Genetic/APOE and risk architecture
weaknesses: Genetic association does not identify one sufficient treatment target.

hypotheses.claim_boundary: PET, CSF, and blood biomarkers strongly identify AD biology but are not equivalent to full clinical causality.
hypotheses.title: Biomarker-defined Alzheimer biology
weaknesses: Baseline overweights biological staging as if it resolves etiology.

hypotheses.claim_boundary: Older-adult dementia often involves overlapping AD, vascular, LATE/PART, and other pathologies.
hypotheses.title: Integrated mixed-pathology model
weaknesses: Baseline does not yet make this a top bottleneck.

hypotheses.claim_boundary: Synaptic/cholinergic dysfunction is clinically relevant but not enough as a disease-origin explanation.
hypotheses.title: Cholinergic and synaptic failure
weaknesses: Symptom response does not prove upstream disease modification.

starting_uncertainty_boundary: Alzheimer disease etiology remains unclear and multi-factorial; the baseline does not treat any hypothesis as solved.
